_meta.name: ALM 14B r7 stack on r8a-fast pod — H1 vs H2 isolation
_meta.task: Re-run r7's stack (unsloth only, dropout=0.05, no liger) on the same fast pod where r8a was measured at 51.4% MFU. Isolates whether the 62.6% vs 51.4% inter-pod gap is from silicon binning (H1) or stack regression (H2).

stack.liger_kernel: 0.7.0 (INSTALLED but NOT ACTIVATED — --liger flag not passed)
stack.note: Identical pip environment to r8a run. Only --liger flag and --lora-dropout value and ALM_COMPILE_UNBLOCK env differ.

smoke_80_dmon.purpose: Run with nvidia-smi dmon in parallel to capture SM util and power telemetry (the 150-step run's dmon was cancelled by a timeout during ssh)

gpu_telemetry.note: Compute-bound: SM 93.4% avg, 100% peak, power 92.9% of TDP. The r7 stack saturates this silicon just as hard as r8a did (95.5% / 93.4% / 100% / 100%) but completes fewer steps per minute — it produces less work per saturated GPU-second.

mfu_computation.formula_basis: Kaplan/Chinchilla 6N FLOPs per training token (fwd+bwd). Same convention as r7 and r8a measurements.

final_cumulative_step150.comment: Cumulative from step 1 — includes Unsloth JIT warmup (step 1 @ 13.0 step/min)

window_step120_150.comment: Derived from cumulative-rate delta 120→150. Post-saturation steady-state.

steady_best_estimate.comment: Asymptotic per-step marginal cost excluding residual warmup

mfu_computation.headline_basis: window_step120_150 — same windowing convention as r8a measurement

comparison_same_pod.direction: r7 stack is SLOWER than r8a stack on this pod

comparison_original_r7_pod.interpretation: Same software stack on two different H100 SXM pods produces 15.3 pp MFU delta. This is PURE silicon/firmware/cooling variance.

H1_silicon_binning.statement: Pod silicon differences dominate the r7 62.6% vs r8a 51.4% gap
evidence: Same r7 stack on two pods → 15.3 pp delta (62.6 vs 47.3)
Same r8a stack slow-pod 48.1% vs fast-pod 51.4% → 3.3 pp delta also pod-driven
Both stacks saturate this pod (SM >93%, power >92% TDP) yet the pod ceiling is ~52% regardless of stack

H2_stack_regression.statement: Adding liger + dropout=0 + compile_unblock in r8a regressed performance vs r7-original
evidence_against: r8a stack on jmejpw 51.4% beats r7 stack on jmejpw 47.3% by +4.1 pp on the SAME pod
The r8a additions (liger + dropout=0) are NET POSITIVE, not negative, under controlled isolation
H2_stack_regression.verdict: REJECTED — the r7 stack is actually the slower one on this silicon

verdict.primary_conclusion: H1 confirmed, H2 rejected. The 62.6% vs 51.4% r7/r8a inter-pod MFU gap is caused by pod silicon variance, NOT by r8a stack regression.
verdict.r8a_stack_recommendation: KEEP — wins by +4.1 pp on controlled same-pod A/B vs r7 stack
verdict.silicon_variance_magnitude_on_runpod_h10: ±15 pp MFU between pods on same stack
verdict.stack_variance_magnitude_on_same_pod: ~±4 pp MFU between r7 and r8a stacks
verdict.actionable_finding: Before any long training run, smoke-test 2-3 candidate pods (40 steps each, ~1 min wall clock) and pick the fastest. Pod shopping delivers ~4x the MFU improvement of stack tuning.
verdict.ceiling_on_this_pod: ~51% MFU bf16 with r8a stack. Gap to 80% practical target: 28.6 pp. No further software lever short of FP8 (blocked by TE wheel on torch 2.10).
verdict.ceiling_on_r7_original_pod: 62.6% MFU bf16 with r7 stack. That pod is terminated; r7 number non-reproducible without pod-hunting.

one_line_verdict: H1 confirmed: silicon binning dominates. r7 stack is actually -4.1 pp slower than r8a stack on this pod; the 62.6% r7 number was pod-specific to the terminated ubzp169igb7tns silicon.
